title: Aerospace Engineer
description: An Aerospace Engineer in India designs, develops, and tests aircraft, spacecraft, and missiles. They work on aerodynamics, propulsion systems, and structural analysis. Their day involves simulations, model testing, and collaboration with teams to meet project deadlines and safety standards.
degree_name: Bachelor of Engineering in Aerospace Engineering
top_private_colleges: Birla Institute of Technology and Science (BITS) Pilani
Sri Sivasubramaniya Nadar College of Engineering (SSNCE)

career_progression.year_0_2: Internships, project work, and basic design tasks under supervision.
career_progression.year_3_5: Design and development of aircraft components, systems, and prototypes.
career_progression.year_6_10: Leadership roles in project teams, system integration, and testing.
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Starting a company focused on aerospace innovation, such as drone technology or satellite manufacturing.
career_progression.global_pathway: Opportunities in the US, Europe, and the Middle East for Indians with relevant experience and skills.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Challenging to find internships and projects, but opportunities exist in major cities.
common_misconceptions: Aerospace engineering is only about designing aircraft.
Aerospace engineers only work in the defense sector.
